text.title: Deliverability Incident Runbook Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules anchoring email deliverability incident response. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Incident detection MUST use numeric thresholds: spam complaint rate > 0.1%, bounce rate > 5%, unsub rate > 2× 30-day baseline. Qualitative triggers ("looks bad") are forbidden.

rationale.source: ESP guidelines (SendGrid, Postmark, Mailgun) + ISP postmaster recommendations

rule.rationale: ISPs themselves use 0.1% complaint rate as a sender-reputation threshold; qualitative triggers cannot be compared across incidents.

rule.statement: Every containment action MUST name a single accountable owner (handle) and an action from {throttle, pause-segment, suppress-list, escalate}. "Team will investigate" is not an action.
Throttle actions MUST be reversible by default (auto-resume in 24h or after warm-up step). Permanent suppression requires escalation to a named human.
rule.rationale: Permanent suppression on the wrong segment kills legitimate revenue; reversible throttle is the lesser harm.

rule.statement: After throttle is lifted, the recovery send MUST follow a documented warm-up curve (10% → 30% → 100% over 3 sends). Skipping warm-up is forbidden.
rule.rationale: ISPs treat sudden volume restoration as a re-attack; warm-up rebuilds reputation.

rule.statement: Every incident report MUST cite at least one diagnostic source (DNS lookup, ESP reputation dashboard URL, complaint sample). Conclusions without sources are rejected.
rule.rationale: Postmortems without sources are guesses; sources make the report reviewable.
